metadata.description: B-spline basis HRF with multi-basis matrix output and spline mathematics

semantic_description.purpose: Validate B-spline basis HRF implementation with matrix output and spline mathematical properties
semantic_description.algorithm:
B-spline basis functions provide a flexible framework for HRF modeling:
**Mathematical Foundation:** - Creates N basis functions using B-splines of specified degree - Each basis function has local support (non-zero over limited intervals) - Basis functions sum to 1 at interior points (partition of unity) - Smoothness determined by degree (degree d → C^(d-1) continuity)
**Implementation Details:** B(t) = splines::bs(t, df=N, knots=knots, degree=degree, Boundary.knots=[0,span])
**Parameters:** - t: Time points to evaluate - span: Temporal window [0, span] (default: 24) - N: Number of basis functions (default: 5) - degree: Polynomial degree (default: 3 for cubic splines)
**Knot Placement:** - Boundary knots at 0 and span - Interior knots placed at quantiles of [0, span] - Number of interior knots = N - degree - 1
**Output:** - Matrix with rows = length(t), columns = N - Each column is one basis function - Values outside [0, span] are set to 0
semantic_description.mathematical_properties:
**B-spline Properties:** - Local support: Each basis function is non-zero over limited interval - Partition of unity: Σ B_i(t) = 1 for t ∈ [0, span] - Non-negativity: B_i(t) ≥ 0 for all i, t - Smoothness: C^(degree-1) continuous - Linear independence: Basis functions are linearly independent
**Boundary Behavior:** - B(t) = 0 for t < 0 or t ≥ span - Smooth transitions at boundaries for degree > 0
**Matrix Properties:** - Dimensions: length(t) × N - Each row sums to 1 for t ∈ (0, span) - Each column has finite support - Matrix is sparse (many zeros due to local support)
semantic_description.edge_cases: - t < 0: Should return zero matrix rows - t ≥ span: Should return zero matrix rows - N < degree + 1: May trigger warnings or adjustments - Single time point: Should return 1×N matrix - Very small span: Should handle boundary conditions - degree = 0: Piecewise constant functions - degree = 1: Piecewise linear functions

input.name: time_vector
input.description: Time points from 0 to 24 seconds with 0.2 second intervals

input.name: extended_time
input.description: Extended time including negative and beyond-span values

input.name: custom_params
input.description: Custom parameters for different basis size

input.name: interior_times
input.description: Time points strictly inside the span

implementations.R:
# R reference implementation # Function: hrf_bspline(t, span=24, N=5, degree=3, ...) # Location: R/hrf-functions.R lines 48-81
# Test default parameters time_vec <- seq(0, 24, by = 0.2) result_default <- hrf_bspline(time_vec, span = 24, N = 5, degree = 3)
# Test custom parameters result_custom <- hrf_bspline(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), span = 20, N = 8, degree = 2)
# Test linear B-splines result_linear <- hrf_bspline(seq(0, 24, by = 1), span = 24, N = 6, degree = 1)
# Test boundary conditions extended_time <- seq(-2, 26, by = 0.5) result_extended <- hrf_bspline(extended_time, span = 24, N = 5)
# Test specific points interior_times <- seq(2, 22, by = 2) result_interior <- hrf_bspline(interior_times, span = 24, N = 5)
# Test single point result_single <- hrf_bspline(12, span = 24, N = 5)
implementations.Python:
# Python implementation following semantic specification
import numpy as np from scipy.interpolate import BSpline
def hrf_bspline(t, span=24, N=5, degree=3): """ B-spline basis HRF with matrix output.
Args: t: Time points (scalar or array) span: Temporal window [0, span] (default: 24) N: Number of basis functions (default: 5) degree: Polynomial degree (default: 3)
Returns: Matrix with rows=len(t), columns=N containing basis functions """ t = np.asarray(t, dtype=float).flatten()
# Handle edge case: if N < degree + 1, adjust effective_N = max(N, degree + 1)
# Create knot vector # Number of interior knots n_interior_knots = max(0, effective_N - degree - 1)
if n_interior_knots > 0: # Interior knots at quantiles interior_knots = np.linspace(0, span, n_interior_knots + 2)[1:-1] else: interior_knots = np.array([])
# Full knot vector: degree+1 boundary knots on each side knots = np.concatenate([ np.repeat(0, degree + 1), interior_knots, np.repeat(span, degree + 1) ])
# Create B-spline basis matrix n_time = len(t) basis_matrix = np.zeros((n_time, effective_N))
for i in range(effective_N): # Create coefficient vector (1 at position i, 0 elsewhere) coeffs = np.zeros(effective_N) coeffs[i] = 1.0
# Create B-spline object and evaluate bspline = BSpline(knots, coeffs, degree, extrapolate=False) basis_values = bspline(t)
# Handle extrapolation (set to 0 outside [0, span]) basis_values = np.nan_to_num(basis_values, nan=0.0) basis_matrix[:, i] = basis_values
# Ensure values outside [0, span] are exactly zero outside_mask = (t < 0) | (t >= span) basis_matrix[outside_mask, :] = 0.0
# Return only requested number of basis functions return basis_matrix[:, :N]
